description_long: This clock tower is a tall, rectangular brick structure with a prominent base made of darker stone, transitioning to a red brick shaft adorned with three vertical, arched recesses on each visible side. The upper section features a slightly projecting cornice and a dark, weathered, hipped roof with a shallow pitch. The brickwork is detailed and uniform, with the arches adding a touch of classical ornamentation. The base is robust and visually separated from the shaft by a stone ledge, suggesting structural solidity. No clock faces or visible openings are present, and the overall design is reminiscent of late 19th to early 20th-century public architecture.
description: A tall, rectangular brick clock tower with arched recesses, a stone base, and a dark hipped roof, lacking visible clock faces.
description_view_0: Front view shows the red brick shaft with three arched recesses and a prominent stone base, topped by a dark hipped roof.
description_view_1: Side view nearly identical to the front, with the same arched recesses and stone base visible.
description_view_2: Rear view mirrors the front, displaying the same brickwork, arches, and roof structure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the three arched recesses and the robust stone base.
